grammarNotes.body: 者 following 兵 forms a topic marker in the topic-comment structure characteristic of Classical Chinese. It selects and foregrounds 'military matters' as the subject of discussion, allowing the subsequent clause to comment on it. Compare with English word order, which would require 'Military (matters) are...' — here, 者 does the grammatical work of nominalization without changing the word itself.

之 functions as a genitive/associative linker here, joining paired nouns (國, 死生, 存亡) to the nouns that follow (大事, 地, 道). This creates abstract noun phrases meaning 'the X of Y.' The pattern 死生之地 and 存亡之道 uses antonymic pairs to form a single conceptual unit: 'the realm of life and death' and 'the principle of survival and destruction.'

不可不察 is a double-negative obligatory construction: 不可 (must not) + 不 (not) + 察 (examine) yields 'must not fail to examine' or simply 'must carefully consider.' The embedded negative neutralizes the prohibition, producing an affirmative obligation. The sentence-final modal particle 也 marks this as an emphatic conclusion — Sunzi is establishing a principle, not describing a particular situation.
